UserAccount.title: UserAccount
UserAccount.description: Represents a user account within the Vibecraft Music application, enabling personalized features like 'Liked Songs'. It stores essential user profile information without sensitive authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserAccount entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:1 AuthenticationSystemUser)

username.description: A display name or handle chosen by the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user account information was updated.

Song.title: Song
Song.description: Represents a single music track, typically sourced from YouTube, containing all necessary metadata for playback and display within the app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Song entity, corresponding to the YouTube video ID.

title.description: The title of the song or track, cleaned and normalized.

artist.description: The artist or creator of the song.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to the high-quality thumbnail image for the song, used as artwork.

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the song in seconds.

publishedAt.description: The date and time when the song (as a video) was originally published on YouTube.

UserLikedSong.title: UserLikedSong
UserLikedSong.description: A junction entity that establishes a many-to-many relationship between a UserAccount and a Song, indicating which songs a particular user has 'liked'.

id.description: Unique identifier for this specific 'liked' relationship entry.

userId.description: Reference to the UserAccount that liked the song. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N UserLikedSong)

songId.description: Reference to the Song that was liked. (Relationship: Song 1:N UserLikedSong)

likedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user liked this specific song.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. The document ID `{userId}` matches the `id` field within the document and the Firebase Authentication `uid` of the user. This collection is private to each user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user account, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores a record of a song liked by a specific user. The document ID `{songId}` is the YouTube video ID of the liked song. The `userId` field is denormalized within the document for authorization independence, matching the `{userId}` in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who liked the song, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: songId
params.description: The unique identifier for the song that was liked (e.g., YouTube video ID). This is also used as the document ID for this entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence:** To achieve authorization independence, the `UserLikedSong` entity denormalizes the `userId` directly into each `UserLikedSong` document. This means that a security rule can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `/users/{userId}` document. This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic. The `UserAccount` entity itself is inherently authorization independent as its document ID directly corresponds to the `request.auth.uid`.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure supports Queryable Authorization Policies (QAPs) by using path-based ownership. 
*   For `UserAccount` data, the path `/users/{userId}` ensures that `list` operations (though less common for individual user profiles) would only be allowed if `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. 
*   For `UserLikedSong` data, the subcollection `/users/{userId}/likedSongs` directly scopes all documents within it to a specific user. When a user queries `/users/current_user_id/likedSongs`, the security rules can efficiently verify that `request.auth.uid == userId` (where `userId` is the wildcard in the path), guaranteeing that only the authenticated user's liked songs are returned. Furthermore, the `songId` as the document ID within this subcollection ensures uniqueness and direct access to a specific liked song.

**Structural Segregation:** Data with different security postures are stored in separate collections. `UserAccount` data is private to the user. `UserLikedSong` data is also private and specific to the user, residing in a subcollection of the user's profile. This avoids mixing data with different access requirements in the same collection, simplifying security rules. 

**Song Entity Handling:** The `Song` entity itself is not stored as a top-level collection in Firestore. This design decision assumes that song metadata is primarily sourced from an external API (like YouTube Data API v3) and fetched on demand. The `UserLikedSong` entity stores only the `songId` as a reference to these external songs, along with the `userId` and `likedAt` timestamp. This keeps the Firestore database lean and avoids duplicating potentially large amounts of external metadata.
